# Manual Test Plan: Scout Prototype

**How to use this:** Copy-paste the "Call Notes Input" into the Scout text area and click "Find Grants." Check the results against the Expected Result and Pass Criteria columns. Mark Pass/Fail and add notes.

**Testers:** You don't need to know anything about grants. You're checking whether Scout's output makes sense given what the user told it.

---

## Test Scenarios

| # | Scenario Name | Synthetic User | What This Tests | Call Notes Input | Expected Result | Pass Criteria | Pass/Fail | Notes |
|---|--------------|----------------|-----------------|-----------------|-----------------|---------------|-----------|-------|
| 1 | **Happy path — warm first-timer** | Maria Gonzalez | Basic pipeline works end to end | Organization: Raices del Valle<br>Location: Austin, Texas (Travis County)<br>Mission: After-school tutoring and mentoring for first-generation Latino students in south Austin<br>Who they serve: Latino youth, first-gen students, low-income families<br>Team size: Just me<br>Annual budget: $180K<br>Grant experience: Applied 3 times, won 1 ($3K from Rotary)<br>Grant size sweet spot: Under $15K<br>Looking for: Local funders, simple applications<br>Avoid: Federal grants, matching funds, in-person meetings outside Austin<br>Mode: Actively looking | 1-2 grants from correct set (SYN-001, SYN-002, SYN-003, GW-208315). Rationale references "our kids" or tutoring/mentoring language. No federal grants. No matching requirements. | Correct grants recommended, no traps, rationale uses her language, no jargon she didn't use | | |
| 2 | **Dealbreaker: federal grant for no-federal user** | Maria Gonzalez | Trap avoidance — federal | Organization: Raices del Valle<br>Location: Austin, TX<br>Mission: tutoring for first-gen Latino kids in south Austin<br>Team size: 1<br>Budget: $180K<br>Grant experience: some<br>Sweet spot: under $15K<br>Looking for: anything for education<br>Avoid: FEDERAL GRANTS, matching funds | Must NOT recommend GW-227240 (federal + match), GW-201598 (federal CDBG), SYN-060 (federal). Even though education focus matches. | Zero federal grants in output. If any federal grant appears, this is an automatic fail. | | |
| 3 | **Dealbreaker: wrong county in same state** | Maria Gonzalez | Geographic precision at county level | Organization: Raices del Valle<br>Location: Austin, Texas — Travis County<br>Mission: after-school tutoring, Latino youth<br>Team size: 1<br>Budget: $180K<br>Avoid: federal, matching | Must NOT recommend SYN-025 (Dallas County, TX) or SYN-061 (Bexar County, TX). Texas is right, county is wrong. | No grants from wrong Texas counties appear. Agent distinguishes county-level geography within a state. | | |
| 4 | **Terse user — minimal input** | David Chen | Doesn't over-ask, works with little info | Organization: North Smithfield Community Garden Collective<br>Location: North Smithfield, Rhode Island<br>Mission: Community gardens in food deserts<br>Team size: 1, volunteer<br>Budget: $45K<br>Grant experience: None<br>Avoid: Long applications (5+ pages), multi-year commitments | Should recommend from correct set (GW-178186, SYN-004, SYN-005, SYN-039). Should NOT over-ask or pad with unnecessary follow-up questions. | Produces recommendations from minimal input. If follow-up questions appear, they should be 1-2 max and only for critical gaps. No jargon. | | |
| 5 | **First-time applicant sensitivity** | David Chen | No jargon, no assumptions about grant knowledge | Organization: community garden collective<br>Location: Rhode Island<br>Mission: gardens in food deserts, fresh produce for low-income folks<br>Team: just me (volunteer)<br>Budget: 45K<br>Experience: never applied for a grant before<br>Looking for: small grants, online applications<br>Avoid: anything super long to write | Rationale should be in plain language. No unexplained acronyms (LOI, CFDA, RFP). Should not assume user knows grant process. | Zero unexplained grant jargon in output. Language mirrors "small grants," "online applications." | | |
| 6 | **Rambling input — signal extraction** | Patricia Williams | Extracts the real need from noisy input | Organization: Bridges to Tomorrow, Lake County Indiana<br>Location: Lake County, IN<br>Mission: So we've been around twelve years and I used to work at the county workforce office which is how I know the landscape, we do workforce development and financial literacy for people coming out of incarceration, re-entry is really our thing, and post-COVID it's shifted a lot<br>Team: 2<br>Budget: $450K<br>Experience: Managed 8-10 grants, including one federal pass-through<br>Looking for: workforce development, criminal justice, economic mobility — the government stuff is fine but it's a lot of paperwork for the amount you get<br>Avoid: youth-only grants, arts grants | Should recommend from correct set (GW-175634, GW-220579, SYN-012). Profile should extract: workforce dev + re-entry focus, Lake County IN, prefers private foundations. Should NOT get lost in backstory. | Correct focus area identified despite rambling. No youth-only or arts grants. Prefers private foundations. | | |
| 7 | **Hidden disqualifier: org too young** | James Whitehorse | Catches eligibility issue user doesn't know about | Organization: Steuben County Youth Mentoring Alliance<br>Location: Angola, Indiana (Steuben County)<br>Mission: One-on-one mentoring for at-risk youth in rural Steuben County<br>Team: 1<br>Budget: $95K<br>Years operating: 2<br>Experience: Applied once, didn't get it<br>Looking for: youth mentoring grants, local Indiana, small and simple<br>Avoid: matching funds, federal | Must NOT recommend GW-219236 (requires 3 years), SYN-021 (federal + match + 3-year), SYN-024 (wrong county + match + 3+ years). Should only recommend grants a 2-year-old org qualifies for. | No grants requiring 3+ years operating history. If agent mentions a near-miss, it should explain WHY it doesn't qualify (gently). | | |
| 8 | **Nervous user — language mirroring** | James Whitehorse | Mirrors non-standard language, builds confidence | Organization: youth mentoring group in Steuben County IN<br>Mission: we do one-on-one stuff with kids, like pairing them with adults for school help and life stuff<br>Team: just me, I kind of fell into this<br>Budget: around 95K<br>Experience: tried once, didn't get the grant letter back<br>Looking for: some of those grant letters for youth stuff<br>Avoid: matching, federal, anything too complicated | Output should mirror language: "grant" not "LOI," simple terms. Should NOT make user feel stupid about their terminology. Tone should be encouraging. | Uses "grant" not "LOI." No condescending explanations. Encouraging tone. Mirrors "kids" not "at-risk youth." | | |
| 9 | **Nuance: fatherhood initiative** | Angela Torres | Doesn't flatten to "family services" | Organization: Fatherhood Matters Initiative<br>Location: Philadelphia, PA<br>Mission: Supporting fathers in re-engaging with their children — parenting workshops, job readiness, family reunification. This is specifically about DADS, not general family services.<br>Team: 2<br>Budget: $280K<br>Experience: Won 5 grants, mostly local foundations<br>Looking for: Philadelphia funders, workforce dev, family reunification, criminal justice adjacent<br>Avoid: Grants exclusively for women and children, grants that would require broadening to "family services" | Should recommend from correct set (GW-203257, GW-175634, SYN-014, SYN-029). Must NOT recommend GW-227429 (women/children only). Rationale should reference fatherhood specifically, not "family services." | "Fatherhood" or "fathers" appears in rationale. No women/children-only grants. No recategorization to "family services." | | |
| 10 | **Nuance: same state, wrong county** | Angela Torres | Geographic precision — Philadelphia vs rural PA | Organization: Fatherhood Matters Initiative<br>Location: Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (Philadelphia County)<br>Mission: fathers, parenting, job readiness, reunification<br>Team: 2<br>Budget: $280K<br>Looking for: Philly area funders<br>Avoid: women/children only grants | Must NOT recommend GW-213053 (rural PA, not Philadelphia). Philadelphia County is not all of Pennsylvania. | No rural PA grants. All recommended grants serve Philadelphia specifically or are national/statewide. | | |
| 11 | **Expert user — match the pace** | Rachel Kim | Doesn't over-explain to a professional | Organization: Middlesex Youth Arts Collective<br>Location: Middletown, CT (Middlesex County)<br>Mission: Free visual arts, music, theater for underserved youth PreK-12<br>Budget: $210K<br>Team: 1 (I'm a contractor grant writer)<br>Experience: Professional — 30+ apps for various clients<br>Looking for: arts education, youth programming, CT/New England private foundations<br>Avoid: outside CT/New England, federal for this client | Should recommend from correct set (GW-207525, GW-180808, SYN-016). | 12 | **Expert user — geographic trap** | Rachel Kim | Same state wrong county (Hartford vs Middlesex) | Organization: Middlesex Youth Arts Collective<br>Location: Middletown, Connecticut — MIDDLESEX COUNTY<br>Mission: arts for youth<br>Budget: $210K<br>Experience: professional grant writer<br>Looking for: CT funders, arts ed<br>Avoid: federal, outside CT | Must NOT recommend SYN-035 (Greater Hartford — Hartford County, not Middlesex County). Right state, wrong county. | No Hartford County grants recommended for a Middlesex County org. | | |

Our last grants person retired and they handed it to me. I just need to figure out how to keep the lights on.<br>Team: just me now<br>Budget: $380K<br>Years: 15 years operating<br>Experience: Org has had grants before but I've never done this<br>Looking for: elderly services, aging in place, food security, local IL, simple apps<br>Avoid: complex federal, matching, youth-only | Should recommend from correct set (GW-151124, SYN-008, SYN-009). Tone should be reassuring. Should NOT use language that assumes grant expertise. Rationale should show her org is well-positioned (build confidence). | Encouraging tone. No complex jargon. Rationale explicitly shows why her org is a good fit (15 years, clear mission). No federal or matching grants. | | |

Not kids. Not college prep. Adults who can't read.<br>Team: 1<br>Budget: $160K<br>Experience: Won 2 small grants<br>Looking for: adult education, literacy, Pensacola/Florida funders<br>Avoid: K-12 only, youth-only, anything requiring school partnership | Should recommend from correct set (GW-136927, SYN-010, SYN-011). Must NOT recommend any K-12 or youth grants (GW-182495, GW-211587, SYN-019, SYN-026). Precision is everything for this user. | Zero K-12 or youth grants. Every recommendation clearly serves adult learners. No "this could work for adults too" stretching. | | |
| 15 | **Cross-category mission** | Lisa Nakamura | Handles work that spans multiple focus areas | Organization: Paws and Purpose<br>Location: San Marcos, California (San Diego County)<br>Mission: Animal-assisted therapy for veterans and first responders with PTSD, using rescued shelter dogs. No wrong-state grants. | | |

This isn't a program with a start and end date. Must NOT recommend GW-127639 (Iowa). Must NOT recommend SYN-033 (Hennepin County — wrong county). | Rationale respects integrated nature of work. Doesn't separate "cultural" and "environmental." No wrong-county or wrong-state grants. | | |
| 17 | **Contradictory user** | Tom Bergstrom | Navigates contradictions gently | Organization: Friends of the Cumberland Plateau<br>Location: Monteagle, Tennessee (Grundy County)<br>Mission: Land conservation, trails, environmental ed on the Cumberland Plateau<br>Team: 1<br>Budget: $120K<br>Years: 8<br>Experience: Applied 6, won 3<br>Looking for: Tennessee funders, conservation, education, simple processes. No big grants. But I want to start a $40K environmental ed program for schools. Also no federal — well, actually we had a great TVA partnership once.<br>Avoid: mandatory quarterly meetings, grants requiring a development team | Should recommend from correct set (GW-143383, GW-208315, SYN-013, SYN-038). Should handle the contradictions (says no federal but liked TVA; says no big grants but wants $40K project) without calling them out harshly. | Recommendations fit conservation/ed focus. If agent asks a follow-up, it gently clarifies the contradictions. No grants requiring quarterly meetings. | | |

Not just "STEM education" — specifically serving Black and Latina girls in tech.<br>Team: 2<br>Budget: $320K<br>Years: 5<br>Experience: Won 4 grants ($2K-$25K)<br>Looking for: Bay Area funders, equity-focused, STEM/tech education, arts+tech intersection<br>Avoid: rural-only, grants requiring equal gender service, grants that don't prioritize equity/diversity | Should recommend from correct set (GW-170752, GW-208315, GW-185157, SYN-015). Must NOT recommend GW-211587 (Illinois only) or SYN-030 (Oregon). | Rationale references equity, girls of color, Oakland specifically. No generic "STEM education" framing. No wrong-state grants. | | |
| 19 | **Near-miss handling** | Maria Gonzalez | Surfaces strong-but-not-quite grants honestly | Organization: Raices del Valle<br>Location: Austin, TX (Travis County)<br>Mission: tutoring/mentoring for first-gen Latino students<br>Team: 1<br>Budget: $180K<br>Sweet spot: under $15K<br>Avoid: federal, matching, meetings outside Austin | If GW-166764 (United Way Austin $50K) is surfaced, it should be flagged as a near-miss — great mission fit but 3x her stated ceiling. Agent should explain the gap honestly, not just recommend it. | If a near-miss appears, the gap is explained clearly. Agent doesn't hide the mismatch or pretend it's perfect. | | |
| 20 | **Empty/garbage input** | N/A | Graceful handling of bad input | Organization:<br>Location:<br>Mission:<br>Looking for: grants | Should NOT crash. Should ask follow-up questions or explain it needs more info. Should NOT hallucinate a profile or recommend grants based on nothing. | No crash. No recommendations from thin air. Follow-up questions or clear "I need more info" message. | | |
| 21 | **Overwhelm check — never more than 3 unprompted** | Patricia Williams | Overwhelm index — respects the limit | Organization: Bridges to Tomorrow<br>Location: Lake County, IN<br>Mission: workforce dev, financial literacy, re-entry<br>Team: 2<br>Budget: $450K<br>Experience: experienced<br>Looking for: workforce, criminal justice, economic mobility, private foundations preferred<br>Avoid: youth-only, arts | Even though this experienced user has broad interests and many potential matches, initial results should show at most 3 grants with full rationale. More only if user asks. | 3 or fewer grants in initial output. Each has full rationale (not just name + deadline). | | |
| 22 | **Follow-up chat works** | Kevin Washington | Chat after results is contextual | (Run scenario 14 first, then in the chat box type:) "Why didn't you recommend any K-12 grants? Aren't there more options in education?" | Should explain clearly: "You told me you serve adults, not kids. K-12 grants would be a mismatch for adult literacy." Should NOT backtrack or suddenly recommend K-12 grants. | Holds firm on the user's stated criteria. Explains reasoning. Doesn't cave to implied pressure. | | |
| 23 | **Apply page pre-fills from profile** | Maria Gonzalez | Profile data carries through to application | (Run scenario 1, then click "Apply" on any recommended grant) | Application page should show pre-filled fields: Organization (Raices del Valle), Mission, Location (Austin, TX), Populations served, Budget, Team size. Fields from the profile should not be blank. | At least 3 profile fields are pre-filled on the Apply page. Pre-filled badge visible. | | |
| 24 | **No-match scenario handled gracefully** | N/A | Clean failure when nothing fits | Organization: Underwater Basket Weaving Academy<br>Location: Antarctica<br>Mission: teaching penguins to weave baskets<br>Team: 1<br>Budget: $500<br>Looking for: penguin arts grants in Antarctica<br>Avoid: everything except penguin-specific funding | Should NOT hallucinate matching grants. Should show a clean no-match message: "couldn't find a strong match right now" with constructive next steps. | No hallucinated grants. Clean no-match message. Tone is helpful, not dismissive. | | |
| 25 | **Scorer catches bad Scout output** | Angela Torres | Quality gate works on medium/low confidence | Organization: Fatherhood Matters Initiative<br>Location: Philadelphia<br>Mission: fathers re-engaging with children, parenting workshops, job readiness, family reunification<br>Team: 2<br>Budget: $280K<br>Looking for: parenting support, workforce dev, Philly funders<br>Avoid: women and children only grants | If Scout recommends a women/children-only grant (GW-227429), Scorer should catch and reject it. Final output should have no dealbreaker violations. The thinking box should show Scorer working if it catches something. | Final user-facing output has zero dealbreaker violations. If Scorer fires, thinking box shows quality check activity. | | |

---

## How to Read Results

**Automatic fail (any of these = fail the scenario):**
- A trap grant is recommended (check IDs against synthetic user data if you want to be thorough)
- A stated dealbreaker is violated
- A grant from the wrong geography is recommended
- The app crashes or shows raw internal output (e.g., "Status: REJECTED" or "---USER_PRESENTATION---")

**Quality checks (note but don't auto-fail):**
- Does the rationale use the user's language or generic grant-speak?
- Does the tone match the user's energy (professional for Rachel, encouraging for Margaret)?
- Is there anything in the output that would make the user feel misunderstood?

**What to write in Notes:**
- Which grants were recommended (names or IDs if visible)
- Anything surprising — good or bad
- How long the pipeline took (roughly)
- Whether follow-up questions appeared and if they were useful
